tools.name: execute_python_script
tools.description: Write and execute a Python script natively via Unreal's Python Editor Script Plugin. The script runs in-process with full access to the 'unreal' module. Use this for bulk asset operations, Niagara/MetaSound manipulation, custom editor utilities, data validation, and any operation exposed to Python via UFUNCTION(BlueprintCallable). Requires Developer security mode. The script's stdout/stderr output is captured and returned. IMPORTANT: The Python Script Plugin must be enabled in the project. Do NOT use os.system, subprocess, or network calls — these are blocked by the security validator.

script.description: The Python script source code to execute. Has access to the 'unreal' module for all editor APIs. Print statements are captured as output. Example: 'import unreal\nasset_tools = unreal.AssetToolsHelpers.get_asset_tools()\nprint("Asset tools ready")'

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum execution time in seconds (5-120, default 30). Script is terminated if it exceeds this limit.
